title: acalypha parvula var. flaccida
intro: Acalypha parvula var. flaccida, also known as the small copperleaf, is a variety of the acalypha parvula plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. The leaves of this plant are used in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties. The health benefits of Acalypha parvula var. flaccida include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to aid in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves of this plant can be made into a tea or infusion by boiling them in water, and it can also be used in traditional remedies to make a poultice or ointment for topical application.
